mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains unusually low, the equity discount rate declines and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if easing-rate periods do not produce higher forward returns than non-easing periods.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS easing periods do not deliver higher 20-day SPX returns than non-easing periods, or if the sign of the return edge is negative for two consecutive 3-year rolling windows, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which lowers expected corporate sales and pushes S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest immediately after the labor deterioration appears.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not lower than the unconditional average by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST indicates demand cooling across consumption and housing, which should depress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism requires both series to weaken together rather than either one alone.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and HOUST slowdowns do not outperform either single-series signal in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal has a positive average return over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is accelerating while CPIAUCSL is not, real activity is improving without an inflation shock, supporting margin stability and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This should fail when output gains are accompanied by accelerating prices.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with flat-to-mild CPIAUCSL inflation do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above trend makes the market expect tighter policy and a higher discount rate, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon inflation surprise effect.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by > 0.2%
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration events are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return after triggers is not below zero across any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS supports household income and consumption, so sustained monthly gains should coincide with positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should weaken if payroll growth is merely a one-month spike and not part of a sustained trend.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll gains do not improve 20-day SPX returns versus the unconditional baseline, or if the sign of the average conditional return flips negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts together with stronger retail sales indicate broad domestic demand momentum, which should support corporate revenues and lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not valid if only housing or only retail sales improve.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined HOUST-RSAFS improvement does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than either series alone, or if the conditional return mean is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE signals a healthy real-economy expansion with improving utilization and labor absorption, which should be bullish for S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should fail when industrial growth is not accompanied by labor improvement.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.2pp over 2 months while INDPRO rises
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up and UNRATE-down regime does not yield positive 20-day SPX returns, or if its hit rate is no better than chance over any 3-year rolling window.
